macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin tumbles below $77,000 as ETF outflows and liquidations deepen crypto rout
items.summary: Bitcoin fell into the $76,000 region, with Ethereum, XRP, and Dogecoin also sliding as fear in the market intensified. Coinglass data cited in the report showed 124,022 traders liquidated in 24 hours for $816.06 million, while spot Bitcoin ETFs logged $290.4 million in net outflows and spot Ethereum ETFs saw $65.7 million in outflows.

items.title: Bitcoin extends four-day decline amid macro pressure and risk-off sentiment
items.summary: A market recap published by Binance Square said Bitcoin fell below $77,000 on May 18, marking four straight daily declines, while Ethereum sank toward $2,100 and major altcoins broadly weakened. The report linked the move to weak rate-cut expectations, elevated Treasury yields, and geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Crypto markets face fresh fear as Bitcoin tests lower support levels
items.summary: Market commentary highlighted Bitcoin technical weakness after repeated rejection near key resistance, with traders warning that a breakdown could open downside toward the $50,000 area. The analysis pointed to bear-flag and megaphone-pattern support levels now being watched closely by bulls and bears.

items.title: NewsNow crypto roundup highlights bitcoin-backed insurance, ETF flows, and treasury buying
items.summary: A live crypto news aggregator compiled several notable developments, including reported bitcoin-backed shipping insurance activity in the Strait of Hormuz, Strategy's large Bitcoin purchases, and continued ETF-related volatility. It also flagged multiple project and treasury updates across Ethereum, XRP, and Solana ecosystems.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Virtuos says it wants to bring GTA 5 and Red Dead Redemption 2 to Switch
items.summary: Port specialist Virtuos said it would like to work on bringing major Rockstar titles such as GTA 5 and Red Dead Redemption 2 to Nintendo Switch. The company previously handled the Switch port of L.A. Noire, underscoring its console-porting experience.

items.title: Subnautica 2 sells 2 million in 12 hours amid huge player surge
items.summary: Subnautica 2 launched to strong demand, reportedly selling 2 million copies in just 12 hours. The early access release also drew more than 600,000 concurrent players, making it one of the week’s biggest gaming launches.

items.title: Two unannounced Xbox controllers leak, including Elite Series 3 and a Cloud pad
items.summary: New Xbox controller hardware appears to have surfaced ahead of any official announcement, including a possible Elite Series 3 and a controller aimed at cloud gaming. The leak suggests Microsoft may be preparing a broader refresh of its accessory lineup.

items.title: Best Buy affiliates told GTA 6 pre-orders start next week, fueling trailer speculation
items.summary: A report says Best Buy has told affiliates that GTA 6 pre-orders will begin next week, prompting speculation that Rockstar or Take-Two may be preparing a new trailer or marketing beat. The timing lines up with Take-Two’s upcoming financial results.

items.title: Nintendo adds more Virtual Boy games to Switch Online
items.summary: Nintendo’s latest update has reportedly added most of the Virtual Boy’s game library to Nintendo Switch Online, with five new titles available now. The rollout continues Nintendo’s steady expansion of its classic-game offerings.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Jury rules against Elon Musk in OpenAI lawsuit over timing of claims
items.summary: A jury ruled against Elon Musk, finding he waited too long to sue Sam Altman and OpenAI. The decision is a notable legal setback in one of the tech industry’s highest-profile disputes.

items.title: Microsoft is testing an adjustable taskbar and Start menu
items.summary: Microsoft is experimenting with UI changes for Windows, including an adjustable taskbar and updated Start menu behavior. The test suggests continued refinement of core Windows navigation and productivity features.

items.title: Google Pixel 3D emoji for Android 17 leaked
items.summary: New leaks show more of Google’s 3D emoji design work for Android 17. The images offer an early look at cosmetic changes expected for the next Android release.

items.title: Boox built an e-reader that resembles the Kindle Scribe concept
items.summary: Boox has introduced a new e-reader that appears to deliver a feature set similar to what Amazon’s Kindle Scribe was expected to offer. The device is drawing attention as a notable move in the e-reader market.

items.title: OpenAI adds bank-account linking for ChatGPT Pro users
items.summary: OpenAI is rolling out a feature that lets ChatGPT Pro users connect bank accounts, expanding the product’s personal finance capabilities. The move points to a broader push to make ChatGPT more useful for financial management tasks.

items.title: OpenAI previews personal finance features in ChatGPT Pro
items.summary: OpenAI has begun previewing finance-related tools inside ChatGPT Pro. The update expands the platform beyond general chat into more specialized consumer assistance.

items.title: Samsung’s Exynos 2800 may deliver stronger on-device performance
items.summary: Samsung’s next Exynos chip is reportedly set for major upgrades that could improve on-device capabilities. The development is significant for Samsung’s future phones and its chipset strategy.

items.title: Linux developers face a wave of AI-generated bug reports
items.summary: Linux maintainers are dealing with a surge of low-quality bug reports that appear to be AI-generated, prompting frustration from project leaders. The issue highlights growing software-maintenance problems tied to automated submissions.
